message:
[HIGH] fcp-sdk: fix use-after-move in PollingSupervisor::run

`items` is consumed by `process_fn(items, &mut self.cursor)` on the
failure path then re-inspected as `!items.is_empty()` before the cursor
persist. With `let_chains` gated on current nightly this is a hard
compile error in HEAD — the whole workspace fails to build because
every crate transitively depends on fcp-sdk. Switch the post-move check
to the already-captured `item_count` local so the guard stays semantically
equivalent without re-borrowing a moved value.

Severity: HIGH — the working tree did not compile prior to this fix,
blocking all cargo check/test runs on any crate that touches fcp-sdk
(which in practice means every connector and every testkit-consuming
suite).

Discovered while reviewing crates/fcp-testkit mock infrastructure;
needed to unblock `cargo check -p fcp-testkit` so the testkit review
could run.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
